Patch Set 3:

Ping reviewers. I'm interested in thoughts on the lustre_compat model. lustre_compat would mirror the actual kernel tree - pieces of backported code could be found at a similar path as the kernel. I think there's a few benefits:

1) Easier to reason about the diff between new kernels and old vendor kernels.
2) Headers would only differ by "lustre_compat/", so automated translation is easier.
3) All compat code lives in the same top-level directory

Patch Set 3:

Should we also move 'LIBCFS_XARRAY_SUPPORT' out of  lustre-libcfs.m4?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Should we also move 'LIBCFS_XARRAY_SUPPORT' out of  lustre-libcfs.m4?

We could. Some projects use a one-file-per-compat-check model. It definitely would help us find and deprecate old checks.

Maybe this could be done in another patch. This is a long one already. LGTM

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

The only reason for this backport is RHEL7 but that is some time away for its removal. Personally I would wait to touch the autoconf setup until everything is in place. While libcfs is easy to move around the lustre llite changes are another matter.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

parents.subject: LU-18691 quota: quota interop check for 64k page clients

commit.subject: LU-18687 compat: move xarray to lustre_compat
commit.message:
LU-18687 compat: move xarray to lustre_compat

Migrate the backported xarray code to lustre_compat.
Along the way, create the needed build infrastructure
for lustre_compat. Currently, lustre_compat is built
into libcfs.ko.

Eventually, all of the Lustre/LNet compatability code
will live in lustre_compat - maintaining a clear
separation from the functional code in Lustre and LNet.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
